items.summary: In `frontend/src/utils/bleNus.ts`, ensure the ESP32 pipe-delimited BLE string parser splits the incoming string by `|`, then extracts the numeric value after `BAT:`, `LAT:`, and `VAL:` prefixes respectively, returning them as structured fields (e.g., `{ battery: number, latency: number, val: number }`). The parser must handle the exact format `BAT:85|LAT:2050|VAL:450`.

In `frontend/src/contexts/BluetoothContext.tsx`, update the BLE notification handler so that when a notification arrives it invokes the pipe-delimited parser from `bleNus.ts` and stores the resulting `battery`, `latency`, and `val` fields in the context state, replacing any prior ad-hoc parsing of these values.

Update `frontend/src/components/device/BatteryIndicator.tsx` to consume the `battery` value (0–100) from `BluetoothContext` and display it in the Battery card. The card must show the numeric percentage and update live as new BLE notifications arrive.

Update `frontend/src/components/device/ActuationLatencyCard.tsx` to consume the `latency` value (milliseconds integer) from `BluetoothContext` and display it in the Actuation Latency card. The card must show the live value as new BLE notifications arrive.

Update `frontend/src/components/charts/BlinkRateChart.tsx` to feed the `val` field (light level) from `BluetoothContext` into the real-time light level chart. Each incoming BLE notification should append a new data point (timestamp + `val` value) to the chart's rolling data series.
